case.title: MANCUSO v. COMM'N OF INVESTIGATION, NEW YORK

opinion.h1: MANCUSO v. COMM'N OF INVESTIGATION, NEW YORK, 361 U.S. 10 (1959)
opinion.h4: MANCUSO v. COMMISSION OF INVESTIGATION OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 117. Decided October 12, 1959.
opinion.p: Eliot H. Lumbard, Nathan Skolnik and Arnold M. Weiss for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: BIRNEL v. TOWN OF FIRCREST, 361 U.S. 10 (1959)
opinion.p: 361 U.S. 10 BIRNEL v. TOWN OF FIRCREST. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WASHINGTON. No. 182. Decided October 12, 1959.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Thomas R. Garlington, Creighton C. Flynn and Robert R. Briggs for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
